qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL injection in view filters via insufficient blacklist validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies SQL injection in /modules/viste/actions.php via check_query() blacklist bypass in the query POST parameter. The advisory describes SQL injection in /modules/primanota/add.php via the id_documenti GET parameter where comma-separated values are concatenated into SQL IN() clauses without integer validation. Different module, endpoint, parameter, and root cause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary file upload allows remote code execution via PHP file upload
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies an arbitrary file upload vulnerability in Upload::build() allowing PHP file uploads. The advisory describes a SQL injection in the Prima Nota module. Completely different vulnerability class, affected code, and root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described SQL injection in /modules/primanota/add.php via the id_documenti GET parameter. The sql_injection_in_view_filters finding is a different SQL injection in /modules/viste/actions.php with a different root cause (check_query blacklist bypass vs missing integer validation for IN() clause). The arbitrary_file_upload finding is an entirely different vulnerability class.
evidence: Advisory root cause: /modules/primanota/add.php lines 63-67, get('id_documenti') -> explode() -> no integer validation -> implode() into SQL IN() clause at line 306
sql_injection_in_view_filters: /modules/viste/actions.php, check_query() blacklist bypass in query POST parameter - different module, endpoint, parameter, and root cause
arbitrary_file_upload: Upload::build() file type validation absence - different vulnerability class entirely
